summary: I was born in Appleton, Wisconsin, in 1993, which is where my childhood began after my family settled there as refugees. My parents came to Appleton after fleeing from Laos and Thailand due to the aftermath of the Vietnam War. Growing up in Appleton, I have many memories, both happy and sad, that shaped my early years.

I am currently a student at the University of Minnesota, pursuing my education there. Attending this university has been an important part of my life as I continue to grow and learn beyond my childhood experiences. It represents a new chapter in my journey after growing up in Appleton, Wisconsin.

My parents came to the United States as refugees in 1993, the same year I was born, seeking safety and a new life after the Vietnam War. Growing up in the United States, particularly in Appleton, Wisconsin, I experienced both struggles and joys as my family adjusted to a new culture and life. The U.S. is where my story unfolds, from my parents' hard work to my own education.

Before coming to the United States, my parents lived in Thailand as refugees after being forced to leave Laos due to the Vietnam War. They stayed in refugee camps there before finally settling in Appleton, Wisconsin. Thailand was a difficult but necessary stop in their journey to safety and a better life for our family.

The Vietnam War had a profound impact on my family, as it forced the Hmong people, including my parents, to flee Laos and seek refuge elsewhere. This war and its aftermath led to my parents' displacement to Thailand and eventually to the United States. The war's consequences shaped the course of my family's history and my own life.

My parents originally came from Laos, but because of the Vietnam War and the dangers it brought to the Hmong people, they had to leave their homeland. They first relocated to refugee camps in Thailand before coming to the United States. Laos is where my family's story began before the upheaval that changed everything.

My dad worked full-time at a pizza factory called Jack's Pizza when I was a child. He would leave in the afternoon and work until early morning to support our family. His hard work at Jack's Pizza was a big part of our lives and helped us get through tough times.

My mom would take me and my younger siblings to Aldi's for grocery shopping since my dad used the car for work and she didn't know how to drive. We would walk about twenty-five minutes to get there, carrying food stamp coupons to buy what we needed. Aldi's was a familiar place during my childhood in Appleton, where we picked up simple dinners like spicy chicken fingers and apple juice.

My two older brothers, Lia and Meng, often spent their time playing Yu-Gi-Oh! cards with friends rather than joining me and my younger siblings on errands. Yu-Gi-Oh! was a popular pastime for them growing up, and it often kept them occupied while we did grocery shopping. It was part of the everyday life and culture in our family during my childhood.
